mission.title: Install and Configure Rook Ceph Operator
mission.description: Rook Ceph Operator is a cloud-native storage orchestrator for Kubernetes that automates the deployment and management of Ceph storage. It provides self-managing, self-scaling, and self-healing storage services, making it ideal for applications requiring reliable and scalable storage solutions. Key trade-offs include the complexity of Ceph and the need for adequate resources to run the operator effectively.

steps.title: Add Rook Helm Repository
steps.description:
This step adds the Rook Helm repository to your local Helm configuration, allowing you to install Rook using Helm.

```bash

helm repo add rook-release https://charts.rook.io/release

helm repo update

```

> This command adds the official Rook Helm chart repository.
> Updates the local Helm chart repository cache.

steps.title: Install Rook Operator
steps.description:
This step installs the Rook operator in the 'rook-system' namespace using Helm.

```bash

kubectl create namespace rook-system

helm install rook-ceph rook-release/rook-ceph --namespace rook-system --version v1.19.3

```

> Creates a namespace for Rook.
> Installs the Rook Ceph operator.
commands: helm install rook-ceph rook-release/rook-ceph --namespace rook-system --version v1.19.3

steps.title: Create Rook Ceph Cluster
steps.description:
This step creates a Ceph cluster using a custom resource definition (CRD).

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/rook/rook/v1.19.3/deploy/examples/ceph/cluster.yaml

```

> Deploys the Ceph cluster configuration.

steps.title: Verify Rook Ceph Cluster
steps.description:
This step checks the status of the Ceph cluster to ensure it is running correctly.

```bash

kubectl -n rook-system get pod

kubectl -n rook-system get cephcluster

```

> Lists all pods in the rook-system namespace to verify the operator and cluster pods are running.
> Checks the status of the Ceph cluster.

uninstall.title: Uninstall Rook Ceph Operator
uninstall.description:
This step removes the Rook operator and all associated resources. Be aware that this will lead to data loss if the Ceph cluster is not backed up.

```bash

helm uninstall rook-ceph --namespace rook-system

kubectl delete namespace rook-system

```

> Uninstalls the Rook Ceph operator.
> Deletes the namespace and all resources within it.

upgrade.title: Upgrade Rook Ceph Operator
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you have a backup of your data. This step upgrades the Rook operator to a newer version.

```bash

kubectl -n rook-system drain <node-name> --ignore-daemonsets

helm upgrade rook-ceph rook-release/rook-ceph --namespace rook-system --version v1.19.3

kubectl -n rook-system uncordon <node-name>

kubectl -n rook-system get pod

```

> Drains the node to prepare for upgrade.
> Upgrades the Rook Ceph operator.
> Uncordons the node after upgrade.
> Verifies that all pods are running after the upgrade.
commands: helm upgrade rook-ceph rook-release/rook-ceph --namespace rook-system --version v1.19.3

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'failed to create cluster'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This may occur due to insufficient resources allocated to the Ceph cluster.

**Fix:**
Ensure that the nodes have at least 2 vCPU and 4GB RAM available.

troubleshooting.title: Pod in 'CrashLoopBackOff' state
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This can happen if the Ceph configuration is incorrect or if there are insufficient permissions.

**Fix:**
Check the logs of the pod using 'kubectl logs <pod-name>' and verify the configuration.

troubleshooting.title: Ceph cluster status shows 'HEALTH_WARN'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that there are issues with the Ceph cluster, such as insufficient replicas.

**Fix:**
Review the Ceph status with 'kubectl -n rook-system exec -it <ceph-tools-pod> -- ceph status' and address the warnings.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'no available monitors'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the Ceph monitors are not running or reachable.

**Fix:**
Check the status of the monitor pods and ensure they are running with 'kubectl -n rook-system get pod'.

versionNotes.changes: Improved handling of Ceph OSDs and added support for new storage classes.
versionNotes.deprecations: Deprecated the use of legacy storage classes.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: If upgrading from v1.18.x, ensure to migrate your storage classes to the new format.

resolution.summary: You should see all pods in the 'rook-system' namespace in the 'Running' state when you run 'kubectl -n rook-system get pod'.
codeSnippets: helm install rook-ceph rook-release/rook-ceph --namespace rook-system --version v1.19.3

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running with the necessary resources.
